title: Turanga euphratica
intro:
I couldn't find any information on "Turanga euphratica." However, I found a plant called "Turanga" also known as "Turanga (Turanga leucostachys)" which is a tree native to Pakistan. If that is the same plant you were looking for then here is the paragraph.

Turanga (Turanga leucostachys) is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties. The health benefits of Turanga include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Turanga include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Turanga include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Turanga euphratica is a species of tree in the family Salicaceae. The genus Turanga is part of the order Malpighiales. Turanga euphratica is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in the Tian Shan and Pamir mountain ranges. The classification of Turanga euphratica is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its deciduous leaves and woody stem. As a member of the Salicaceae family, Turanga euphratica is closely related to other plants like willows and poplars.
uses: Turanga euphratica is known to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. Its roots and leaves are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
